title: Dryopteris camptocaulon
intro: Dryopteris camptocaulon, also known as the bent-stalk wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris camptocaulon include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Dryopteris camptocaulon is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Dryopteris, which consists of approximately 250 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various types of ferns. Polypodiales is a suborder within the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises the ferns. This classification places Dryopteris camptocaulon within the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Pteridophyta.
